commit.message:
feat: implement full production tech stack — Ethereum, IPFS, BLS, ZK, Redis, Claude, WebSocket

Blockchain (Ethereum / Polygon):
- app/services/ethereum_service.py — Web3.py + AuditTrail.sol (Solidity 0.8.21)
  compiled via py-solc-x, deployed to local EthereumTesterProvider (PyEVM)
  or any RPC URL (Ganache/Polygon/Sepolia via ETHEREUM_RPC_URL env var)
- 5 endpoints: /blockchain/status, /record, /verify, /entry/{i}, /entries
- Every proposal approval now records hash on-chain + emits AuditRecorded event

ZK Proofs (Circom-compatible primitives):
- app/services/zk_service.py — Pedersen commitments (C = G^m * H^r mod P)
  + Merkle inclusion proofs (double-hashed leaves, sibling path)
- 3 endpoints: /zk/commit, /zk/verify-commitment, /zk/merkle-proof

Real BLS12-381 threshold signatures:
- app/crypto/bls_threshold.py already used py_ecc — now wired into approval flow
- 3 endpoints: /bls/keygen, /bls/sign, /bls/aggregate-verify
- Each approval generates a real BLS partial signature (BLS12-381 G2PoP scheme)

IPFS document storage:
- app/services/ipfs_service.py — IPFS HTTP API client with local-fallback
- 3 endpoints: /ipfs/upload, /ipfs/get/{cid}, /ipfs/status

Redis caching + rate limiting:
- app/services/redis_service.py — sliding-window rate limiter, JSON cache,
  WebSocket presence tracking; in-memory fallback when Redis is unavailable

AI: Claude Sonnet + GPT-4 (dual backend):
- app/services/ai_service.py — rewrote to use anthropic SDK for analysis
  (claude_analyze: analyze/summarize/compliance/anomaly) and openai for
  completions/grammar; graceful fallback when keys not configured
- 3 new endpoints: /ai/analyze, /ai/complete, /ai/status

Real-time WebSocket collaborative editing:
- app/main.py — _RoomManager with join/leave/broadcast; /ws/{room_id}?token=
- crypto-auth-system/lib/useCollabWebSocket.ts — React hook with auto-reconnect
- crypto-auth-system/app/requests/page.tsx — LivePresenceBanner component
  shows connected users + fires toast on live approval events

Infrastructure:
- docker-compose.yml — full stack: PostgreSQL, Redis, IPFS (kubo), Ganache,
  backend, frontend with health checks and service dependencies
- requirements.txt — added web3, eth-tester[py-evm], redis, anthropic,
  openai, py_ecc, py-solc-x, websockets
- .env.example — documented all new env vars with examples
- README.md — updated with real stack table, architecture diagram,
  endpoint reference, ZK/BLS/Ethereum technical details

Tests: 27/27 passing, frontend: 26/26 pages build

Add display ir (#234)

* Add mccode_antlr.display submodule for MCDISPLAY geometry extraction

Parse a component's MCDISPLAY section into symbolic Python geometry
primitives backed by Expr, evaluate them with concrete parameter values,
and render the result in 3-D — without compiling the instrument.

New submodule: src/mccode_antlr/display/
  - primitives.py      — Primitive dataclasses (Line, Circle, Box, Sphere,
                         Cylinder, Cone, Rectangle, Multiline, DashedLine,
                         Magnify, ConditionalBlock, LoopBlock); every argument
                         is an Expr; to_polylines(params) → list[np.ndarray]
  - visitor.py         — DisplayVisitor(CVisitor) using the existing C99
                         CParser/CLexer; handles calls, local var declarations,
                         conditionals (→ ConditionalBlock), loops (→ LoopBlock)
  - component_display.py — ComponentDisplay wraps Comp.display RawC blocks
  - instrument_display.py — InstrumentDisplay applies Orient transforms
                            (Rotation × pts + translation, all Expr-backed)
  - render/matplotlib.py — zero-new-dep renderer via mpl_toolkits.mplot3d
  - render/threejs.py    — soft-import renderer for pythreejs / K3D

Also:
  - expose display from src/mccode_antlr/__init__.py
  - add docs/api/display.md + mkdocs.yml nav entry
  - 21 new tests (all passing); full suite 509 passed, 10 skipped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

visitor.py: replace leading-only cast strip with a global

  are removed before Expr.parse() (which uses the McCode grammar).
  Also handle bare assignment statements (name = expr;) inside
  MCDISPLAY blocks as local variable updates.

docs/examples/display_geometry.ipynb: new Jupyter notebook
  demonstrating the full mccode_antlr.display workflow:
    1. parse_display_source() with symbolic args
    2. Local variables + C-style cast resolution
    3. Conditional blocks (if/else guards)
    4. ComponentDisplay on a hand-crafted Comp
    5. Real McStas component (Slit) loaded via load_mcstas_instr()
    6. InstrumentDisplay in the global frame (with ROTATED transform)
    7. Manual Primitive construction + matplotlib rendering

✨ HACKATHON: Email notifications, admin panel overhaul, frontend polish

## New Features
- **Email Notification Service** (app/services/email_service.py)
  - Transactional HTML emails for: proposal created, approval received,
    quorum reached, proposal executed, welcome on user creation
  - SMTP via env vars (SMTP_HOST/USER/PASSWORD); graceful demo-mode fallback
  - New API endpoints: GET/PUT /notifications/preferences,
    POST /notifications/email/test, POST /notifications/email/send,
    GET /notifications/email/status

## Admin Panel Redesign (crypto-auth-system/app/admin/page.tsx)
  - Full AppSidebar + AppHeader layout (was raw unstyled HTML)
  - Stats row: total users, approvers, admins, quorum count
  - Tabbed interface: Users | Keys | Policy | Email
  - Click-to-fill user ID for password/key generation
  - Live policy editor with current state display
  - Email status card with SMTP config instructions + test button
  - Role badges with color coding (admin=purple, approver=blue, viewer=slate)

## Frontend Polish
- All admin dropdowns: approver / viewer / admin (removed invalid 'signer')
- committee/page.tsx: fallback role 'committee_member' → 'approver'
- settings/page.tsx: notification toggles with descriptive subtitles,
  side-by-side quiet hours grid
- all-documents/page.tsx: wrapped in full-page flex container

## Bug Fixes
- lib/state.ts → state.tsx (JSX in .ts file caused TS errors)
- /committee/add|remove: changed query params → Body(...) JSON input
- /approvals/ and /settings PUT: added Body(...) annotation
- Email hooks wired into create_request, approve_request, execute_request

## Theme
- Dark/light/system theme toggle already functional via next-themes
- ThemeProvider confirmed in root layout with CSS variable tokens

commit.message:
fix: 移除浏览器端createDataChannel，改用agent端创建

- 之前浏览器在收到offer后调用pc.createDataChannel()创建新channel
- 这与agent端已创建的channel冲突，导致SDP协商失败
- Agent日志: remote sdp: missing a=ice-ufrag/pwd (因为浏览器answer不完整)
- 正确流程: agent在offer中包含datachannel，浏览器通过pc.ondatachannel接收
- 将DataChannel的onopen/onclose/onmessage逻辑移到ondatachannel处理函数中
- 测试: WebSocket连接正常，等待浏览器端验证

commit.message:
feat: publish composite GitHub Action for KSail cluster provisioning (#3060)

* Initial plan

* feat: add reusable GitHub Actions workflow for KSail cluster provisioning

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add fail-fast error detection in cluster readiness polling

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: convert reusable workflow to composite action

Addresses all review feedback:
- Composite action runs within caller's job so cluster is accessible
- No --ttl backgrounding or disown needed
- No kubectl dependency for readiness polling
- Proper permissions documented

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: enhance ksail-cluster action and refactor system test to use it

Add provider, args, init, install, and hcloud-token inputs to the
ksail-cluster composite action. Refactor ksail-system-test to delegate
cluster init+create to ksail-cluster, eliminating duplicated logic
while maintaining full feature parity.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add free-disk-space and registry pre-pull to ksail-cluster action

Extract universally useful functionality from system test into the
ksail-cluster composite action:

- Add free-disk-space input (default true) that removes unused SDKs
  and prunes Docker to free ~17GB of runner disk space
- Add pre-pull registry:3 step with retries for Docker providers,
  making cluster creation more reliable
- System test passes free-disk-space: false (CI handles cleanup
  before cache restoration) and removes its own registry pre-pull
- Test workflow adds all 4 distributions and cleanup step

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: replace free-disk-space with generic caching and image pre-pulling

Remove free-disk-space from ksail-cluster action and add:

- Helm chart caching via actions/cache keyed by ksail version
- Mirror registry volume caching (save/restore Docker volumes as tar)
- Generic image pre-pulling using ksail workload images CLI (no source
  code dependency) with retries for all distributions

System test passes cache: false since it has its own caching from warm
jobs. The action stays simple for end-users (cache: true by default)
while providing significant speedups on subsequent CI runs.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: make system tests use ksail-cluster caching

Remove redundant cache restoration and VCluster pre-pull steps from
ksail-system-test in favor of ksail-cluster's built-in caching (cache:
true). The ksail-cluster action already handles:

- Helm chart caching via actions/cache (keyed by ksail version)
- Mirror registry volume caching (restore/import/export/save)
- Generic image pre-pulling via ksail workload images (no source code
  dependency, replaces Dockerfile-based VCluster pre-pull)

Removed steps:
- restore-mirror-cache (→ ksail-cluster restore mirror cache)
- verify mirror volumes (→ ksail-cluster import mirror volumes)
- restore-helm-cache (→ ksail-cluster restore Helm chart cache)
- VCluster pre-pull from Dockerfile (→ ksail-cluster pre-pull
  distribution images via CLI)

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: remove redundant test-ksail-cluster workflow

The ksail-cluster action is implicitly tested by system tests,
making the dedicated test workflow redundant.

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments on ksail-cluster action

- Support ARM64 runners via runner.arch mapping (X64→amd64, ARM64→arm64)
- Use inputs.ksail-version directly for cache keys instead of parsing
  ksail --version output
- Fix mirror import/export to only log success when operations succeed
- Parse --kubeconfig from args for accurate kubeconfig output path

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add default case to kubeconfig arg parser to iterate all args

Co-authored-by: devantler <26203420+devantler@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList for proper argument quoting

Start-Process -ArgumentList joins array elements with spaces without
quoting. The copilot prompt argument contains spaces, newlines, and
special characters that got split into multiple arguments, completely
breaking copilot CLI invocations.

Replaced Start-Process with System.Diagnostics.Process using
ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList (a .NET Collection<string>) which
properly quotes each argument per OS conventions. Also reads stdout
asynchronously to prevent deadlocks from buffer fill.

transforming the repository from a presenter-driven demo into an
instructor-led + self-paced hands-on workshop for experienced developers
new to Microsoft Agent Framework.

Workshop structure (27 new files):

  ├── README.md                    — Overview, agenda, setup, exercise map
  ├── exercises/
  │   ├── ex1_run_agent/           — Phase 1 (Foundations): create & run first agent
  │   ├── ex2_web_search/          — Phase 1: add HostedWebSearchTool (Bing grounding)
  │   ├── ex3_mcp_tool/            — Phase 2 (Integration): MCPStdioTool via npx
  │   ├── ex4_structured_output/   — Phase 2: Pydantic response_format + fallback
  │   ├── ex5_workflow/            — Phase 3 (Orchestration): multi-agent WorkflowBuilder
  │   ├── ex6_devui/               — Phase 3: DevUI entity + serve()
  │   └── ex7_capstone/            — Phase 4: build-from-scratch challenge (3 options)
  ├── reference/
  │   ├── agent-framework-guide.md — Deep-dive guide (~670 lines)

  └── instructor/

Exercise design — progressive scaffolding:

commit.message:
fix: resolution/fps runtime change and CAD button

Resolution/FPS fix:
- Root cause: check_video_ctrl() consumed ctrl_rx messages and updated
  the inner function's local cfg_mut, which was dropped on bail. The
  outer loop's cfg was never updated with the new values.
- Fix: encode new config (W:H:FPS) in the bail message, parse in
  outer run_with_ctrl() loop to properly update cfg before restart.
- Also fixed try_hw_h264(): added ctrl_rx parameter, backpressure
  sleep, and video restart support (was completely missing).

CAD button fix:
- Send key sequence in 3 steps with proper timing:
  1. Ctrl+Alt modifiers down (0ms)
  2. +Delete key down (50ms)
  3. All keys released (200ms)
- Previous 30ms single-shot was too fast for many target systems
- Added console logging for debugging

* Refactor twin players: remove unused UI update function and enhance YouTube API mock for testing

* Add TikTok original sync fixture and enhance player mock for testing

* feat: enhance original video metadata handling for TikTok and YouTube

- Added support for fetching and normalizing original video metadata from TikTok and YouTube.
- Updated `CreatorDetails` component to display original video author URL and platform.
- Modified `useTwinPlayers` to include original video metadata in state management.
- Implemented new helper functions for building URLs and fetching metadata.
- Enhanced reaction document creation to include original video details.
- Updated shared session management to handle original video platform and URL.
- Refactored API endpoint for fetching original video metadata.
- Adjusted various routes to utilize the new metadata fetching logic.

* Add playlist sequence support and recorder state

Introduce canonical playlist sequencing and recorder-state utilities, plus related UI, backend and API updates.

Highlights:
- Add src/lib/helpers/reactionSequence.js: canonical sequenceItems schema, parsing, normalization, conversion helpers and playlist assignment logic to support duplicate-safe mixed YouTube/TikTok playlists while remaining backward-compatible with originalVideoIds.
- Add src/lib/helpers/recorderState.js: recorder player state constants and helper to build recorder state configs for storing reaction/original time+state mappings.
- Update Firebase helpers to create/update playlists using sequenceItems (createPlaylistDocument, addToPlaylistDocument, getUserPlaylists) and to use new reactionSequence helpers.
- Update UI components: PlaylistQueue and PlaylistQueueItem now consume sequenceItems, support hiding the current item, stable keys (id), and build canonical navigation params (platform/sequenceIndex/originalUrl). Also use buildYouTubeThumbnailUrl helper.
- Extend useTwinPlayers composable to handle sequenceItems: find current index, populate playlistItems from sequence, preserve reaction timing across consecutive identical originals, reuse/destroy original players correctly, restore iframe containers, and apply richer original metadata.
- Add originalVideo helper buildYouTubeThumbnailUrl and enhance youtube helpers with playlist preview/pagination/fetchAll functions and updated server endpoint to return next-page token and optional playlist title.
- Update backend recorder page to wire sequenceIndex, load sequenceItems for recorder flows, use recorder state helpers to persist reaction state, and adapt TikTok state codes.
- Docs updated (SCHEMA_REFERENCE.md) to document sequenceItems schema and version bump; integration tests added for reaction-sequence and recorder-state.

This change enables reliable ordered playback/recording of playlists that may contain duplicates or mixed platforms while maintaining legacy compatibility.

commit.message:
Retry next queue item when architecture is skipped

When a queue item is classified as architecture, the loop now tries
the next pending item instead of wasting the entire experiment. This
prevents the exp 18 scenario where the only picked item was skipped
and the experiment produced no code change or PR.

The GetNext  classify  architecture-check is now wrapped in a while
loop. Architecture items are still marked done/skipped and logged.
The experiment only skips if ALL remaining queue items are exhausted.
